entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The primary crop species under study for male sterility.

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A condition where pollen production is inhibited due to cytoplasmic factors.

entities.name: CULTIVATED PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The domesticated variety of pigeonpea used as the female parent in CMS development.

entities.name: CAJANUS ACUTIFOLIUS
entities.description: A wild relative of pigeonpea used as the pollen donor to induce CMS.

entities.name: HPL 24
entities.description: A specific pigeonpea cultivar that partially maintained male-sterility.

entities.name: POLLEN FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of the plant to produce viable pollen.

entities.name: CYTOLOGICAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: The study of cellular structures used to investigate the cause of male sterility.

entities.name: MEIOSIS
entities.description: The process of cell division that leads to the formation of spores/pollen.

entities.name: TETRAD STAGE
entities.description: The stage of meiosis where four haploid cells are formed.

entities.name: POLLEN MOTHER CELL WALL
entities.description: The cell wall of the pollen mother cell which must dissolve to release tetrads.

relationships.description: is the crop in which CMS is reported
provided the cytoplasm for the new CMS system
acted as the pollen donor to develop CMS
was used to identify the cause of male-sterility
proceeded normally despite the presence of male-sterility
is a specific stage within the process of meiosis
failure to dissolve is responsible for male-sterility
